title: NSW residential leases: cooling-off period
content: Unlike some consumer contracts, NSW residential tenancy agreements generally do not include a statutory cooling-off period once a lease is signed. If you have not yet signed, you can still negotiate or walk away. If you have signed, your rights to end the tenancy early depend on the lease type (fixed term vs periodic), break-lease clauses, and mutual agreement with the landlord. Always check your written agreement and current NSW Fair Trading guidance.

title: NSW rental bond: maximum amount and lodging with Fair Trading
content: For most NSW residential tenancies, the bond cannot exceed four weeks rent (special rules can apply to very high weekly rents). The landlord or agent must lodge the bond with NSW Fair Trading (Rental Bonds Online is commonly used) within 10 working days after you pay it. You should receive information confirming lodgement. If you are unsure whether your bond was lodged, you can check with NSW Fair Trading.

title: NSW bond return after tenancy ends
content: When a tenancy ends, the landlord or agent generally must apply to Fair Trading to release the bond within 14 days (unless you have already agreed in writing to a deduction). Disputes over how much should be returned can be handled through Fair Trading’s bond claim process and, if needed, NSW Civil and Administrative Tribunal (NCAT). Tenants should provide a forwarding address and keep copies of condition reports and rent records.

title: NSW rent increases: notice and frequency
content: For most NSW residential tenancies, rent can only be increased if the agreement allows it (or after a fixed term ends, depending on arrangement). When an increase is permitted, the landlord must give at least 60 days written notice using the approved form, and in a continuing tenancy increases are generally limited to once in any 12-month period. Exact rules depend on tenancy type and start date; verify against current NSW Fair Trading materials.

title: NSW landlord entry: notice and frequency
content: Except in emergencies, the landlord or agent should give you at least 24 hours written notice before entering for a routine inspection or other non-urgent reason, and entry for inspections is typically limited to at most about once in any 7-day period unless you agree otherwise-always verify against current NSW Fair Trading materials and your written agreement. Emergencies (e.g. burst pipe, gas smell, serious safety risk) can justify immediate entry without ordinary notice.

title: NSW repairs: urgent vs non-urgent
content: Landlords must keep the premises in reasonable repair. Urgent repairs (things that make the property unsafe or essential services unusable) should be addressed quickly-Fair Trading guidance often refers to arranging urgent repairs as soon as possible, and there are statutory pathways for tenants to authorise urgent repairs up to a set amount in defined circumstances. Non-urgent repairs should still be carried out within a reasonable time after notice. Document requests in writing and keep photos where relevant.

title: NSW breaking a fixed-term lease (tenant)
content: Ending a fixed-term tenancy early without grounds can mean you are responsible for break fees or the landlord’s reasonable costs to re-let (such as advertising and a letting fee proportionate to the remainder of the term), subject to the landlord’s duty to mitigate loss. Mutual surrender (agreed in writing) is often the cleanest path. If you are experiencing serious hardship or certain domestic violence circumstances, different rules may apply-seek specialist tenancy advice.

title: NSW notices to vacate: tenants and landlords (overview)
content: Notice periods depend on whether the agreement is fixed term or periodic, who is giving notice, and the reason. Tenants typically give 14 days for a periodic tenancy or 21 days to end at the end of a fixed term (confirm current forms and minimum periods). Landlord notice periods vary widely (for example 30 to 90 days or more) depending on the grounds and tenancy type. Use the official NSW notice forms and dates; wrong notice can invalidate the process.

title: NSW discrimination, children, and pets in renting
content: Anti-discrimination law limits some reasons a landlord may refuse a tenant. Refusing applicants because they have children is generally not lawful in NSW rental housing contexts covered by discrimination protections. Pets are different: landlords may refuse pets unless the tenancy agreement or strata rules and current rental law say otherwise; recent reforms have shifted some defaults-check current NSW Fair Trading guidance on pets in rentals and any strata by-laws.

title: NSW: if the landlord will not return the bond or claims unfair deductions
content: If the landlord or agent delays lodgement, withholds the bond without grounds, or claims deductions you dispute, start by checking Rental Bonds Online status and gather your ingoing/outgoing condition reports, photos, rent ledger, and messages. You can dispute a bond claim through Fair Trading’s processes. If agreement cannot be reached, NCAT can decide bond disputes. For non-lodgement, contact NSW Fair Trading-bonds must generally be lodged within the statutory timeframe.

title: NSW: making a complaint to Fair Trading (tenancy)
content: NSW Fair Trading handles many rental compliance issues (bond lodgement, some agent conduct, and consumer information). You can lodge a complaint online or by phone (13 32 20). Have your address, dates, agency details, and evidence ready. Fair Trading may investigate compliance or mediate in some matters. Tribunal applications (NCAT) are separate and may be needed for orders about possession, rent, or bond disputes that are not resolved by agreement.

title: NSW: landlord or agent enters without proper notice (student and general renters)
content: If someone enters without lawful notice or consent (and it is not a genuine emergency), document the date/time, who entered, and any evidence (messages, photos, witnesses). You can complain to Fair Trading about agent conduct in some cases. Repeated or serious breaches may support tribunal applications about breach of quiet enjoyment. Avoid retaliatory actions; use written communication and official pathways. For tailored advice, contact Tenants’ Union of NSW or your student legal service.

title: NSW bond deductions and reasonable wear and tear
content: At the end of a tenancy, landlords may claim bond money only for lawful amounts-typically unpaid rent or damage beyond reasonable wear and tear. Fair wear and tear means deterioration from ordinary daily use (e.g. minor carpet flattening, small scuffs) rather than negligence, stains, holes, or broken items. The ingoing and outgoing condition reports are critical evidence. If deductions seem unfair, dispute the claim via Fair Trading/NCAT with photos and records.

title: NSW condition reports and why they matter
content: At the start and end of a tenancy, a condition report records the property’s state. In NSW, the landlord/agent must provide an ingoing report; tenants should complete the tenant section within the required timeframe and keep a copy. Disputes about cleaning or damage often turn on this document plus photos and time-stamped communications.

title: NSW smoke alarms and basic safety obligations
content: Landlords must ensure smoke alarms meet NSW rules (type, placement, maintenance). Tenants must not remove or interfere with alarms and should report faults promptly. If alarms are missing or faulty, request repair in writing and escalate via Fair Trading guidance if needed. This summary is general-check current NSW legislation and Fire and Rescue NSW guidance.

title: NSW locks, keys, and security devices
content: Tenants have rights around security. Changing locks can require landlord consent except in certain safety circumstances; provide copies of keys where required. If you are excluded unlawfully or locks are changed without process, seek urgent advice. Rules are detail-heavy-confirm with Fair Trading or a tenancy adviser.

title: Quni platform fees for students (booking and weekly)
content: On Quni's current managed pricing model, students pay $0 platform fee (including booking fee and weekly platform fee). Exact presentation can vary by product flow and should be confirmed at checkout and in your agreement.

title: Quni landlord fees and listing cost
content: Landlords can choose Listing tier (flat fee per accepted booking, currently $99) or Managed tier (weekly percentage service fee, currently 7%). Creating a listing remains free; fees are applied according to the selected service tier and current pricing configuration.

title: Quni student verification requirements
content: Students typically complete verification using a university email OTP, photo ID, and proof of enrolment. Verification helps landlords trust applicants and may be required before certain actions (such as booking or payments). Requirements can change-follow the in-app checklist.

title: Quni non-student renters and identity checks
content: Renters who are not students may use a separate identity verification tier appropriate to their profile. The goal is consistent trust and safety on the marketplace. See onboarding prompts for the exact documents and steps.

title: Quni host verification (Verified host badge)
content: Why landlords use Stripe for identity (and what students should know): Quni's core trust feature for renters is the Verified host badge. Before any host can accept a booking - Listing or Managed - they must complete Stripe Connect identity verification. Stripe (a regulated payments provider) performs the KYC checks (such as government ID and business details). Quni does not manually review landlord profiles, verify host government IDs in-house, or check property titles. When Stripe confirms the Connect account can accept charges (charges_enabled), Quni shows the Verified host badge on the host profile and on listings. That badge is the main signal students should look for when asking whether a landlord is legitimate - it means the host passed Stripe identity checks before they could accept anyone. Important timing: students can browse listings, message the host, and submit a booking request before the host finishes Stripe. Only accepting the booking is blocked until verification completes - so lack of a badge during enquiry does not mean the platform skipped checks; it means acceptance has not happened yet. If Stripe later restricts the account, the badge can be removed. Listing tier acceptance also requires a saved card for the flat platform fee charged on accept. Direct hosts to landlord dashboard → Rent payouts / Connect onboarding to complete verification.

title: Quni payouts via Stripe Connect
content: Quni uses Stripe Connect for landlord payouts on Quni Managed tenancies. After renters pay weekly rent through the platform, the Managed service fee is deducted and the remainder is paid out to the landlord's connected bank account. Transfers typically settle in about 2–3 business days depending on bank processing, public holidays, and Stripe timelines. Landlords must finish Connect onboarding (including identity verification) before they can accept Managed bookings and receive payouts. On Quni Listing, weekly rent and bond usually flow directly between landlord and renter after acceptance - Quni is not in the ongoing rent payment chain for Listing. Ensure Connect shows charges enabled in the landlord dashboard to avoid accept/payout blockers.

title: Quni student payments and Stripe (deposit, rent, fees)
content: Student and renter payments on Quni: Renters pay no Quni booking fee, platform fee, service fee, or surcharge to Quni - bond and weekly rent are tenancy money between renter and landlord, not Quni revenue. Booking application (/booking/:propertyId): step includes a Stripe booking deposit (typically around one week's rent plus any amounts shown at checkout). Paying the deposit submits the request; if the landlord declines, the authorised hold is released or refunded via automated Stripe flows - often 5–7 business days back to card or bank. Rent method: choose Quni card (saved to Stripe Customer on the student profile for recurring rent on Managed paths) or bank transfer where offered. Bond: acknowledged in the booking flow; actual bond handling depends on property type (lodged with state authority for many residential tenancies, or held by landlord for hosted/boarder-style arrangements) - Quni is not the bond custodian. Look for the Verified host badge on listings for landlord identity trust (Stripe KYC before accept). Student verification (uni email, photo ID, enrolment or identity docs) is separate from host verification and helps landlords assess applicants.

title: Quni landlord payments and Stripe (Listing vs Managed)
content: Landlord payments and Stripe by service tier. Quni Listing: creating listings is free. On accept, Quni charges a flat platform fee per accepted booking from the landlord's saved card via Stripe (3-D Secure when required). After acceptance, bond and weekly rent are handled directly between landlord and renter - Quni does not collect ongoing rent on Listing. Quni Managed: landlords use Stripe Connect for identity verification and for weekly rent collection; the Managed service fee is deducted from rent before payout to the landlord's bank (see payouts article). Both tiers require Stripe Connect identity verification (charges_enabled) before the landlord can accept any booking - this powers the Verified host badge students rely on for peace of mind. Landlords can list, receive messages, and receive booking requests before finishing Connect; only accept is gated. Onboarding: Listing landlords save a card in the payments step; Managed landlords complete Connect in onboarding and via dashboard Rent payouts. Managed-tier properties may block new booking deposits until Connect is ready; Listing-tier often allows renter deposits before the host finishes identity. Refunds on decline follow automated Stripe rules for the renter deposit hold.

title: Quni dispute resolution between users
content: If a dispute arises between a student and landlord on Quni, parties should first communicate in good faith through the platform and keep a written record. For payment, booking, or account issues, contact Quni support at hello@quni.com.au with relevant IDs and screenshots. Matters that are really residential tenancy disputes (bond, possession, repairs) are usually handled under NSW tenancy law via Fair Trading or NCAT, not by Quni as a substitute for those agencies.

title: Quni AI and legal disclaimer
content: Quni AI provides general information only, not legal advice. Laws and policies change, and your situation may differ. For specific legal problems, contact NSW Fair Trading on 13 32 20 or the Tenants’ Union of NSW. For urgent safety issues, contact emergency services.

title: NSW: rent in advance and rent payment records
content: Landlords may ask for rent in advance within legal limits at the start of a tenancy. During the tenancy, keep receipts or electronic records for every payment. If amounts or dates disagree, written records are essential for tribunal proceedings.

title: NSW: sub-letting and assignment
content: You generally cannot sub-let or assign your tenancy without the landlord’s consent, and the landlord must not unreasonably withhold consent in many standard residential cases-exceptions and details depend on your agreement and current law. Always get agreements in writing before handing keys or taking money from a sub-tenant.

title: NSW: water usage charges and utilities
content: Whether a tenant pays water usage depends on whether the property meets efficiency standards and what the lease says. Electricity and internet are commonly tenant accounts unless otherwise agreed. Read your lease and any disclosure materials; dispute unclear charges in writing.

title: Quni sample agreement previews (landlords and students)
content: Quni publishes watermarked sample PDFs of its live agreement templates so hosts and renters can see layout and wording before a booking is confirmed. While logged in, open Sample agreements from the dashboard link or go directly to /sample-agreements. You can browse by state (NSW, QLD, VIC) and tier: T1 occupancy or licence-style documents, and T2 residential agreements plus the Quni platform addendum where applicable (for example NSW FT6600, QLD Form 18a, VIC Form 1). Click a thumbnail to open a full preview or download the PDF. These files are marked SAMPLE - not for execution; they are not your signed lease. After a booking is confirmed, Quni generates the real agreement package for that listing. To refresh samples after template changes, the team runs npm run generate:agreement-samples.

title: Quni tenancy documents by property type and state
content: The agreement type depends on listing property type and state. On-site room with landlord living on premises is often T1 (occupancy or licence-style). Off-site room, entire property, or shared room without rooming-house registration is often T2 (formal residential tenancy). NSW T2 uses the FT6600-style residential agreement plus a Quni addendum; QLD T2 uses Form 18a plus addendum; VIC T2 uses Form 1 plus a VIC addendum. VIC T1 uses a licence to occupy. Preview all current samples on /sample-agreements when logged in, or the full matrix on /admin/agreement-previews for internal review.

title: Quni shared product features (students and landlords)
content:
Source: docs/feature-inventory.md (living product inventory). Live = shipped end-to-end. UI only = shown in the app but not fully backed - say "coming soon" if asked. Deprecated = replaced. Do not invent features or routes not listed here.

- Peer messaging per listing (`/messages`, `/messages/:conversationId`)
- Unread badge in header when logged in as student or landlord
- Contact masking until booking accepted; unlock shows email/phone
- Public listings browse and property detail (`/listings`, `/properties/:slug`) with role-specific gates
- Verified host badge on listing cards and property detail when `landlord_profiles.verified` (Stripe-driven)
- AI chat widget (persona: student renter vs landlord); host verification honesty rules + knowledge-base chunk
- Sample agreement previews (`/sample-agreements`) - watermarked PDF templates by state/tier; dashboard link for students and landlords
- Auth: signup, login, Google OAuth, email verification, sign out

### Trust, Stripe & payments - Live

- Verified host (core trust feature): landlords complete Stripe Connect identity verification (regulated KYC by Stripe, not manual Quni ID review) before they can accept any booking; when Stripe enables charges, a Verified host badge shows on profile and listings so students get peace of mind
- Students may browse, message, and submit booking requests before a host finishes Stripe - only acceptance is gated on verification
- Students: no Quni booking/platform/service fees; booking deposit via Stripe (card hold/charge at application); ongoing rent via Quni card (Stripe Customer) or bank transfer; bond is tenancy money between parties (Quni is not the bond custodian)
- Landlords - Quni Listing: saved card for flat acceptance fee (charged on accept via Stripe); bond and weekly rent flow directly with the renter after accept (Quni not in rent chain)
- Landlords - Quni Managed: Stripe Connect for identity + weekly rent collection; service fee deducted before payout to bank (~2–3 business days typical)
- Declined bookings: deposit hold released/refunded via automated Stripe flows (often 5–7 business days)
- Legal/info: Terms, Privacy, Refunds, How it works, FAQ, Contact
- Ad-hoc feedback via Sentry (`submitUserFeedback` in `src/lib`) - no public “Report a problem” button; structured issues use Qase (admin + dashboard “Get support”)

---

title: Quni student and renter product features and routes
content:
Source: docs/feature-inventory.md (living product inventory). Live = shipped end-to-end. UI only = shown in the app but not fully backed - say "coming soon" if asked. Deprecated = replaced. Do not invent features or routes not listed here.

### Account & auth - Live

- Sign up as Student or Non-student tenant (same `student` role; different verification route)
- Email/password + confirmation email; Continue with Google (role chosen first)
- Login, resend confirmation, OAuth callback (`/auth/callback`)
- Role/terms gate (`/onboarding`); legacy `/student-signup` → `/signup`
- Delete account (confirm with `DELETE`)

### Onboarding (`/onboarding/student`) - Live

- University email OTP (student route only at onboarding); non-students skip uni email and verify later on Profile → Verification
- Steps: about you → emergency contact + living prefs → accept Terms/Privacy
- Profile photo; uni/campus/course/year; phone; budget; move-in; lease length; gender
- Draft saved in browser (resume / start fresh); back between steps

### Profile (`/student-profile`, `/student/profile`) - Live

- Tabs: Profile | Verification | Bookings (`?tab=…`)
- Edit profile, photo, emergency contact, living preferences
- Work location + geocode (non-student route) for distance search
- Rent billing card - Stripe Customer for ongoing rent (Quni card path)
- Profile draft autosave
- Delete account (danger zone)

### Verification - Live

- Student route: uni email OTP, photo ID, enrolment proof
- Non-student route: work email OTP, photo ID, identity supporting doc
- Progress meter (Verification tab + onboarding)

### Dashboard (`/student-dashboard`) - Live

- Cards: bookings (+ pending), messages, profile completeness, browse CTA
- Dismissible onboarding checklist with deep links
- Tabs: Bookings | Messages (link) | Saved
- Get support (Qase modal)
- Stripe payments card; NSW tenancy copy + lease panel when applicable
- Download bond receipt (where listing type supports it)
- View sample agreements → `/sample-agreements`
- Legacy `?tab=enquiries` → `/messages`

### Saved listings - UI only

- Saved tab shows “coming soon”; no backend favourites yet

### Search & browse - Live

- Filters (URL-synced): keyword, university/campus, suburb, room type, rent, furnished, dates, lease length
- Near work: `near_lat` / `near_lon` / radius; nearest first sort
- Use my saved work location (non-student with coordinates saved)
- Sort: newest, price low/high, nearest
- Date-aware availability badges; adjust dates when unavailable
- Banner when non-student must verify to see all student-only listings
- Post-onboarding welcome on `/listings`
- SEO: university/campus accommodation, rent near campus
- Featured listings on Home (guest)

### Property detail - Live

- Guest: partial listing + sign-in CTA
- Student-only listings access check (RPC)
- Gallery, amenities, rules, nearby campuses/listings
- Share listing
- Verified host label when landlord has completed Stripe identity (badge on cards; inline on detail)
- Message landlord (conversation); pending intent after onboarding
- Request to book (gated until core profile complete)
- Link if active pipeline booking exists
- Property enquiry form - Deprecated (redirects to Messages)

### Booking application (`/booking/:propertyId`) - Live

- Student role only; 4 steps: dates & occupancy → rent method → bond ack → deposit payment
- Move-in (min 7 days), lease length, 1–2 occupants, co-tenant fields when 2
- Optional parking surcharge; message to landlord
- Rent: Quni card or bank transfer
- Bond acknowledgment; Stripe booking deposit (1 week + platform fee when applicable)
- Booking draft in localStorage; date overlap / conflict UI
- Cannot book own listings (if user is also landlord)
- Listing-tier properties: deposit/booking request allowed before host finishes Stripe identity; Managed-tier blocked until host Connect ready
- Statuses: `pending`, `pending_payment`, `pending_confirmation`, `awaiting_info`, `bond_pending`, `confirmed`, `active`, `declined`, `expired`, `payment_failed`, `cancelled`, `completed`, etc.

### Messaging - Live

- Inbox; realtime thread; mark read; send messages
- Masked contact until acceptance; then landlord name, email, phone
- Open from listing (`openConversation`)

### Post-booking - Live

- Lease panel: draft preview, sign (DocuSeal), download signed agreement + addendum
- Co-tenant signing awareness
- Reply when status is `awaiting_info` (profile Bookings tab)

### Payments - Live

- Booking deposit (Stripe); saved card for rent; method stored on booking

### AI & support - Live

- AI chat (`student_renter` persona)
- Qase tickets from dashboard
- Contact page (student enquiry type)

### Gating (reference)

| Requirement | Unlocks |
|-------------|---------|
| Email confirmed | Protected routes |
| Core profile (uni, course, phone, budget) | Message + book |
| `onboarding_complete` | Full onboarding flow done |
| `verification_type === 'student'` | All student-only listings (non-student route sees subset + prompt) |

---

title: Quni landlord product features and routes
content:
Source: docs/feature-inventory.md (living product inventory). Live = shipped end-to-end. UI only = shown in the app but not fully backed - say "coming soon" if asked. Deprecated = replaced. Do not invent features or routes not listed here.

### Account & auth - Live

- Signup as landlord; tier intent from pricing (`?tier=listing|managed` → localStorage)
- `/landlord-signup` → `/signup`; post-auth → onboarding then `/landlord/dashboard`
- Sign out; email verification

### Onboarding (`/onboarding/landlord`) - Live

1. Profile - photo, name, phone, type (Individual / Company / Trust), ABN, address, bio  
2. Terms - Terms, Privacy, Landlord Service Agreement  
3. Payments - Listing: save card (+ optional Connect); Managed: Stripe Connect (identity verification required before accept, not before listing)  
4. Insurance - insurer links + confirmation checkbox  
5. Complete - add first listing or dashboard  
- Wizard draft autosave; Stripe return query params

### Dashboard (`/landlord/dashboard`) - Live

- Onboarding checklist; Add new listing (gated until setup)
- Rent payouts (Connect): connect / continue / manage
- Stats: listings, messages (unread), bookings (pending), profile %
- Get support (Qase); Managed Connect banner when needed
- View sample agreements → `/sample-agreements`
- Tabs: Listings | Messages | Bookings

### Listings (dashboard & profile Properties tab) - Live

Per listing:

- Edit (`/landlord/property/edit/:id`)
- View public `/properties/:slug`
- Share listing (disabled while draft)
- Publish (draft → active)
- Duplicate → confirm → new draft (`duplicate_property_listing`)
- Pause / Reactivate (active ↔ inactive)
- Badges: draft/active/inactive, featured, service tier

### Property form (`/landlord/property/new`, edit) - Live

- Sections: Basic info, Property details, Inclusions, Rules, Location, Description, Pricing, Photos
- Types: Rent, Homestay, Student House
- Accommodation tiles; beds/baths; room for rent; rooming house registration
- Inclusions (furnished, linen, cleaning, features); Open to non-students
- House rules (Yes/No/Approval) + custom; Reset to platform default
- Location + geocode; suggested campuses; extra universities
- Description; AI description generator
- Quni Listing vs Quni Managed; rent/week; AI price suggestion; occupants; couple/carpark surcharges; bond; lease length; available from
- Photos: upload, reorder, captions, remove
- Publish listing / Save changes
- New listing: draft indicator; resume draft / start fresh

### Booking review (`/landlord/bookings/:bookingId/review`) - Live

- Fit summary; occupancy/rent; verification badges; read-only thread
- AI assessment (generate / refresh, cooldown; all applicant tiers - student, identity, incomplete)
- Accept as Listing / Accept as Managed / Upgrade and accept as Managed
- Decline (optional reason); Request more information (suggested chips + message)
- Bond received from renter (Listing); Cancel booking
- Boarding/lodger: Mark bond received → generate bond receipt
- Tenancy agreement: draft, sign, download + addendum
- Blockers: Stripe identity (`stripe_charges_enabled`), Listing saved card, billing module
- Verified host badge (Stripe-synced; admin manual override with lock)

### Bookings tab (dashboard) - Live

- Request queue; 48h expiry; Review request; applicant modal
- Verification details; AI assessment; download/open agreement
- Bank account required modal; payment error retry

### Landlord profile - Live

- Tabs: Profile | Properties (same listing actions)
- Completion widget; account agreements; rent payouts; saved Listing card display
- Edit profile + photo

### Messaging - Live

- Same shared messaging; landlord as host; contact unlock after booking

### Host identity & trust - Live

- Verified host badge when Stripe Connect has `charges_enabled` (webhook + sync; flips off if Stripe disables)
- Accept booking gated on Stripe identity for both Listing and Managed; Listing also needs saved card for $99 fee
- List, message, and receive booking requests without verification; only accept requires Stripe
- Admin manual verified toggle sets `admin_override_verified` so webhooks do not overwrite

### Payments & payouts - Live

- Stripe Connect (Managed rent payouts; host identity KYC)
- Listing fee card; charge on accept via `/api/confirm-booking` (3DS when needed)
- Refund deposit on decline; Listing cancel with fee rules

### Service tiers - Live

- Quni Listing - self-managed; card fee on accept; bond/rent with renter; optional upgrade to Managed on accept
- Quni Managed - Connect required; managed workflow; no downgrade after upgrade

### AI & support - Live

- AI chat (landlord persona)
- Qase from dashboard

### Marketing / leads (pre-login) - Live

- Landlord partnerships + lead form → `landlord_leads`
- Landlord AI page; pricing CTAs with tier; Landlord Service Agreement

title: Quni NSW landlord address on residential tenancy agreement
content: Whether a landlord's home address appears on the signed residential tenancy agreement depends on the Quni service tier. On Quni Listing, the landlord self-manages the tenancy without a managing agent. Under NSW residential tenancy law, the landlord's address for service of notices must appear on the agreement in that situation, so the landlord's home address is included on the lease. On Quni Managed, Quni acts as the appointed managing agent. Service-of-notices details use Quni's business address instead of the landlord's home address, which does not appear on the lease. The landlord's name and a contact telephone number still appear on the agreement as NSW law requires, and if the landlord lives outside NSW, their state or territory is noted. This applies to NSW residential tenancies (for example FT6600-style agreements). Quni Managed availability varies by location.
